title: High-Performance Protocol for ONT Library Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"hkdqh","text":"This protocol presents a refined version of the Oxford Nanopore Technologies (ONT) Ligation Sequencing amplicons V14 (SQK-LSK114) protocol, optimized for the sequencing of short and ultra-short DNA fragments. It maintains the use of reagents required for standard ONT library preparation while enhancing DNA yield and improving sequencing performance. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1o8hn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ccmb","text":"The protocol can be successfully used for all types of Flow Cells, but the Flow Cell priming and loaded library volumes must be adjusted accordingly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"93q89","text":"Thaw all the reagents and keep them on ice throughout the protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"24tub","text":"Prior to use, thoroughly mix all components by vortexing, except for the End-Prep Enzyme Mix and Quick T4 DNA Ligase.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9sbje","text":"Perform all centrifugation steps at room temperature.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bq67k","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3o7fm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":4,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"fetds","text":"Note: \"ONT\" refers to reagents from the standard SQK-LSK114 kit provided by Oxford Nanopore Technologies.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

steps: Transfer 250 fmol of the dsDNA to a clean 0.2 mL PCR tube, adjusting the volume based on DNA concentration.
Add the following components sequentially, mixing by pipetting approximately 10 times after each addition: %s %s
Briefly centrifuge the tube and place it into a thermal cycler with a heated lid.
Run the following thermal cycling program: 20 °C for 00:05:00 . 65 °C for 00:05:00 .
After incubation, transfer the reaction mixture to a 1.5 mL RNase- and DNase-free low-bind tube.

Add the following components in the specified order, mixing by pipetting approximately 10 times after each addition: A B 1 Component Volume 2 End-prepped DNA 60 µL 3 Ligation Buffer (LNB, ONT) 25 µL 4 Quick T4 DNA Ligase (E6056S, NEB) 10 µL 5 Ligation Adapters (LA, ONT) 5 µL 6 Total volume 100 µL
Incubate the reaction at Room temperature for 00:20:00 .
Add AMPure XP Beads (AXP, ONT) to the reaction tube based on the DNA length: 180 µL for fragments < 100 bp. 50 µL for fragments > 100 bp.
Mix by inverting the tube for 00:05:00 .
Briefly centrifuge the tube, then place it on a magnetic rack for 00:03:00 to pellet the beads.
While keeping the tube on the magnet, carefully remove the supernatant.
Add 250 µL of Small Fragment Buffer (SFB, ONT) to the tube.
Remove the tube from the magnet and gently rotate for at least 00:03:00 , occasionally flicking to ensure even bead distribution.
Briefly centrifuge the tube and place it back on the magnet for 00:03:00 , then remove the supernatant.
Repeat the wash step with 250 µL of SFB following the same procedure.
After the final wash, briefly centrifuge the tube, then place it on the magnet to remove any remaining supernatant.
Allow the bead pellet to dry for 00:01:00 before removing the tube from the magnet.
Add 12 µL of Elution Buffer (EB, ONT) to the tube and mix thoroughly by pipetting.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube back on the magnet for 00:03:00 to pellet the beads.
Transfer the supernatant containing the prepared library to a new 1.5 mL RNase- and DNase-free tube and place On ice .
Measure DNA concentration using the Qubit HS dsDNA kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
Insert a Flongle (ONT) flow cell into a MinION Mk1B device (ONT).
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 before initiating sequencing run.
Perform a quality check using MinKNOW software.
Select only flow cells with ≥ 50 active pores for sequencing.
Prepare the flow cell priming mix by combining the following components in a 1.5 mL tube, then immediately place On ice : %s %s
Peel back the seal tab of the Flongle flow cell to expose the sample port.
Secure the seal tab by adhering it to the MinION lid using its adhesive strip.
Carefully load the priming mix into the flow cell, ensuring no air bubbles are introduced.
Incubate the flow cell for 00:05:00 .
During the incubation period, prepare the sequencing mix by combining the following components: %s %s
creator: Rūta Palepšienė
